query: What demand charges apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in Chicago, based on the OpenEI tariffs data? Answer should contain service class name and demand charge for at least 2 classes
final_answer:
# Demand Charges for Commercial Customers >500 kW in Chicago (Commonwealth Edison)

Based on the OpenEI IURDB tariff data for **Commonwealth Edison Co** serving Chicago, IL, here are the relevant service classes and their demand charges for larger commercial customers:

## Service Classes Applicable to >500 kW Demand

| Service Class Name | Voltage Category | Demand Charge ($/kW/month) | Fixed Charge ($/month) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **BESH-Medium Load Delivery Class (Unbundled)** | Primary | **$7.47969/kW** | $43.00 |
| **BESH-High Voltage Delivery Class (Unbundled)** | Primary | **$8.25969/kW** | $889.42 |
| **Medium Load Delivery Class (Retail Delivery Service-Unbundled)** | Primary | **$3.49/kW** | $43.00 |

## Key Details

1. **BESH-Medium Load Delivery Class (Unbundled)** – This class is designed for medium-to-large commercial loads at primary voltage. The flat demand charge is **$7.47969/kW** per month, with an energy rate of $0.01168/kWh plus adjustments. *(Source: [OpenEI rate view](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/539fc4b6ec4f024c27d8ca11))*

2. **BESH-High Voltage Delivery Class (Unbundled)** – This class is specifically for large customers taking service at high/primary voltage levels. The demand charge is **$8.25969/kW** per month, with a notably higher fixed charge of $889.42/month reflecting the larger service requirements. *(Source: [OpenEI rate view](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/539fc89aec4f024d2f53e8ba))*

3. **Medium Load Delivery Class (Retail Delivery Service-Unbundled)** – A standard unbundled delivery class at primary voltage with a lower demand charge of **$3.49/kW** per month. *(Source: [OpenEI rate view](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/539f7193ec4f024411ece7a5))*

## Important Notes

- The **"BESH"** prefix denotes **Bundled Electric Service Hourly** pricing, which includes real-time hourly energy supply charges (available from ComEd's pricing tool).
- The distinction between BESH and standard unbundled rates affects how energy supply costs are structured—BESH rates have lower base energy charges but layer on hourly market pricing.
- All tariffs are from **Commonwealth Edison Co** (EIA ID: 4110), the utility serving Chicago.
- Demand charges are applied to monthly billing demand (typically based on the highest 15-minute or hourly interval during the billing period).

For a >500 kW customer, the **Medium Load** and **High Voltage** delivery classes are the most relevant, as the Small Load and Watt-Hour classes are designed for smaller commercial customers.
